items.title: Claude’s voice mode is now available for Opus and Sonnet
items.raw_content: Until now, voice mode has only been available on Claude Haiku, Anthropic's faster but less powerful model. Now the company is making its Opus and Sonnet models available in voice mode, and extending its reach into apps like Gmail, Slack, and Canva. When Anthropic launched voice mode last year, it was primarily focused on delivering […]

items.title: Anthropic updates Claude voice mode with more capable models
items.raw_content: Claude's new voice model will let you reschedule your meeting or draft an email.
items.summary: Claude's new voice model will let you reschedule your meeting or draft an email.

items.title: Runway launches AI model router as generative media gets crowded
items.raw_content: The Media Router is a tool that automatically selects the best image, video, or audio generation model for a request based on whether a developer prioritizes quality, speed or cost.
items.summary: The Media Router is a tool that automatically selects the best image, video, or audio generation model for a request based on whether a developer prioritizes quality, speed or cost.

items.title: Meta launched a new AI optimism ad set to a song about human extinction
items.raw_content: David Bowie's song "Five Years," which Meta used in a supposedly inspiring advertisement, is about humans learning that they have five years left to live before the apocalypse.
items.summary: David Bowie's song "Five Years," which Meta used in a supposedly inspiring advertisement, is about humans learning that they have five years left to live before the apocalypse.

items.title: Google’s Gemini nears billion-user milestone
items.raw_content: Gemini had over 750 million monthly users in February.
items.summary: Gemini had over 750 million monthly users in February.

items.title: Alexa Plus is getting an AI update to handle more complicated instructions
items.raw_content: Amazon is launching an update to its Alexa Plus assistant that will allow it to connect to smart home devices in new ways. With the update, which is currently in preview, Alexa Plus can link up with tech from Bosch, Delta, Ecovacs, iRobot, Yale Home, Whirlpool, Tapo, Eufy, and others, while automatically routing requests to […]

items.title: How AI guardrails are impeding the work of offensive cybersecurity researchers
items.raw_content: We spoke with several cybersecurity researchers, who look for unknown vulnerabilities and develop tools to exploit them, about how OpenAI’s and Anthropic’s guardrails affect their work.
items.summary: We spoke with several cybersecurity researchers, who look for unknown vulnerabilities and develop tools to exploit them, about how OpenAI’s and Anthropic’s guardrails affect their work.

items.title: OpenAI makes ChatGPT Health available to all US users
items.raw_content: Users can also integrate their personal data from services like Apple Health, Function, and MyFitnessPal.
items.summary: Users can also integrate their personal data from services like Apple Health, Function, and MyFitnessPal.

items.title: OpenAI is making big claims as it rolls out ChatGPT Health to everyone
items.raw_content: OpenAI is rolling out ChatGPT Health to everyone in the US on Thursday, allowing more people to connect their medical records and health-tracking information to the chatbot. During a briefing, Ashley Alexander, OpenAI's vice president of health product, says the company's models "are now capable of reasoning at levels that are better than clinician level." […]

items.title: AegisAI, founded by former Google security execs, lands $36M to stop AI-driven spear phishing
items.raw_content: AegisAI co-founders developed AI agents that quickly analyze each message as a human would, paying attention to small anomalies that even the most elaborate checklist wouldn’t catch.
items.summary: AegisAI co-founders developed AI agents that quickly analyze each message as a human would, paying attention to small anomalies that even the most elaborate checklist wouldn’t catch.

items.title: Apple’s OpenAI lawsuit is about who gets to define the post-smartphone era
items.raw_content: Today on Decoder, I’m talking with Hayden Field, The Verge’s senior AI reporter, about the major trade secrets lawsuit between Apple and OpenAI and what this tells us about OpenAI’s future. By now I’m sure most Decoder listeners are familiar with Apple’s allegations in this case. The company says a number of ex-Apple employees at […]

items.title: AMD commits up to $5 billion to  Anthropic
items.raw_content: AMD says it's going to invest up to $5 billion in Anthropic, while helping to expand the AI company's computing power, according to an announcement on Wednesday. As part of the new partnership, Anthropic will deploy up to 2 gigawatts of AMD's Instinct MI450 AI GPUs using the chipmaker's new Helios rack-scale system, as reported […]

items.title: AMD takes on Nvidia with its Helios AI rack-scale system
items.raw_content: AMD is challenging its chipmaker rival with a new rack-scale system that will start shipping to customers later this year.
items.summary: AMD is challenging its chipmaker rival with a new rack-scale system that will start shipping to customers later this year.

items.title: AI chip startup Etched defies skeptics, hits $10.3B valuation from big-name investors
items.raw_content: Etched, founded by three Harvard dropouts, has created new chips and memory components that speed up inference on any AI model -- no GPUs required, it says.
items.summary: Etched, founded by three Harvard dropouts, has created new chips and memory components that speed up inference on any AI model -- no GPUs required, it says.

items.title: Patreon is laying off 20 percent of workers
items.raw_content: Patreon is laying off 20 percent of its workers, or around 93 employees, as reported earlier by 404 Media. In a memo to employees, Patreon CEO Jack Conte writes that the company isn't making these changes "because we believe AI replaces humans," but says AI has "fundamentally transformed the tech industry, including how we work, […]

items.title: Lawmakers prepare bill requiring AI ‘kill switch’
items.raw_content: Lawmakers are preparing to introduce an "AI Kill Switch Act" that would require AI companies to shut down or throttle their systems on orders from the Department of Homeland Security, according to a report from Politico. Reps. Ted Lieu (D-CA) and Nathaniel Moran (R-TX) are expected to introduce the legislation on Thursday. The news of […]

items.title: Nvidia is sending GPUs to the moon
items.raw_content: If there's a place in the universe without GPUs, Nvidia is sending them there.
items.summary: If there's a place in the universe without GPUs, Nvidia is sending them there.

items.title: The right-wing boomers protesting data centers have a lot in common with the left
items.raw_content: On a gray, humid Saturday morning in central Florida, a little under a dozen people gathered outside the Spring Hill Branch Library to protest the construction of a hyperscale data center in their community. There was no immediate threat - the Hernando County commission had unanimously approved a one-year moratorium on such developments in June […]

items.title: Here’s what Samsung’s smart glasses actually look like
items.raw_content: Samsung has given us our first chance to check out its upcoming smart glasses in person, revealing two new designs and the first specs in the process, including an impressive 9-hour battery life. items.title: Meta made its own AI detection system. It should have just used Google’s
